MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ricardo Enriquez appeared by zoom for California Department of State Hospitals.
Kevin Dwight appeared by zoom for Fremont Bank (trustee).
Steve Picone apppeared by zoom for Donald Amos.

Judge Moody disclosed that prior to being appointed to the bench he worked at the Attorney General's Office for 21-years, that the does not believe his prior employment will impair his ability to be fair and impartial, and that he never worked in this section and never met Mr. Enriquez. Mr. Dwight requested a recess to have a discussion with his client. Upon re-calling of the matter, Mr. Dwight stated that he is prepared to go forward. Mr. Enriquez and Mr. Dwight provided oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Motion for Summary Judgment is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reported: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977, sheilapham@gmail.com

The following matters were heard together: PTR-14-297928 Douglas P. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297929 Douglas P. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297930 Donald J. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297931 Donald J. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Kevin Dwight, Ana Guardado and Vivian Redsar for Fremont Bank.
Charles Antonen for The Attorney General's Office on behalf of the Department of State Hospitals.
Barbara Zweig (Representative of the Department of State Hospitals) also present.

The Court informed the Parties that it cannot resolve the matter today, that the amount of the claim is unresolved and that the petition needs to be resolved all at once. Discussion was held as to the scope of discovery to be conducted and as to what issues (statute of limitations, the terms of the trust, the commitment issue). Mr. Dwight stated that he intends to conduct discovery on all three issues. The Court stated that it expects discovery to be conducted expeditiously. The Parties are to file a joint status report regarding discovery, any ADR proceedings, the trial plan/how long it will be, the factual issues to be tried, the witness list with time estimates of testimony, etc. The Court informed Counsel that it will not go more than four months out. Mr. Dwight stated that he wants to make sure there is enough time to do a motion for summary judgment. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation in case number 297928 only, but mediation applies to all four cases. Mediation must be completed no later than Aril 1, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation and file their joint status statement by April 10, 2020. The Petitions for an Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Directing Payment of the California Department of State Hospitals (4) are continued to and the Parties are to return on April 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065, chreporter@comcast.net

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For An Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Directing Payment Of The California Department Of State Hospitals Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles J. Antonen and Barbara Hiyama Zweig, counsel for State of California, Dept. of State Hospitals ("DSH"); Kevin Dwight and Ana Guardado, Counsel for Fremont Bank, Successor Trustee.

Court in session, on the record. Counsel state their appearances. At the Court's invitation, Mr. Dwight addresses the Court's tentative ruling, noting that the trusts' terms specifically exclude payment or reimbursement of public benefits, notes that Probate Code Sec. 15306 only allows "judgment creditors" to pierce spendthrift clauses and provisions and that there is no judgment here. Mr. Dwight and the Court discuss the Trustee's collateral estopple argument.

Mr. Antonen responds, Court and counsel review the ramifications of the special needs trust, and Mr. Dwight replies.

The Court takes the matter under submission.

Mr. Antonen notes that the DHS Petitions are to be heard on Monday [August 26, 2019] and inquires as to whether those hearings should be continued pending the Court's ruling on the instant demurrers. He also notes a pending request for pro bono mediation. The Court defers consideration at this time.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Raquel G. Sharp, CSR# 10619, 925-384-0003, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-14-297928 Douglas P. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297929 Douglas P. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297930 Donald J. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297931 Donald J. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Sheldon Wolfe for Bank of America.

Mr. Wolfe reported that Douglas Amos died on 3/24/15, that they had made an allocation and moved funds, that petitions to be filed on behalf of Fremont Bank will request that funds be transferred, that he is not aware if Douglas Amos had an estate plan, that he does not know if Donald Amos has an estate plan either although he does have an attorney, that Donald has 3 children who are estranged and that Douglas' estate will pass to Donald and then to the children of Donald. The Court stated that there would be an issue of attorney's fees it they were before the court, that there is no request for approval of fees, that there have been no objections, that the beneficiaries are not in a position to be active and that the court can allow the accounting without acting on or approving any attorney's fees. Mr. Wolfe stated that the bank said they had paid the fees and he thought they were appropriate and that there will be petitions filed in the next couple of days by Fremont Bank. The Court confirmed that the petitions will include orders transferring to Donald's trust. The Court granted the accounting petitions in all 4 matters (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Settling First And Final Account And Report Of Trustee For Douglas P. Amos Gst Ex
Off Calendar Jan-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together: PTR-14-297928 Douglas Amos Exempt Trust, PTR-14-297929 Douglas Amos Non-Exempt Trust, PTR-14-297930 Donald Amos Exempt Trust & PTR-14-297931 Donald Amos Non-Exempt Trust.
Appearances: Sheldon Wolfe for (petitioner) Bank of America. Ernest Der as Guardian Ad Litem for Douglas Amos.

The court signed the orders accepting resignation of trustee and appointing successor trustee and informed counsel that the orders denying the petitions for modification of the trusts were not received. Orders were provided by Mr. Wolfe in open court. The court once again raised the issue of whether there is any way to improve Douglas' quality of life. Mr. Der addressed allocation of fees stating that the bulk of the fees were approved by the court and that the proposed adjustment is fine. Mr. Wolfe indicated that the final accounting period for Bank of America is from January 2014 through January 27, 2015. All accountings are allowed subject to review of the order and the allocation matter being reflected in the next final accounting. Mr. Der stated that he will speak with the social worker at Napa State Hospital. The court would like to see a declaration regarding what may be done for Douglas. Status on filing of Bank of America's final account was set for March 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Settling First And Final Account And Report Of Trustee For Douglas P. Amos Gst Ex
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Continued To Jan-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Settling First And Final Account And Report Of Trustee For Douglas P. Amos Gst Ex
Off Calendar Dec-23-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297928 The Douglas P. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297929 The Douglas P. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297930 The Donald J. Amos GST Exempt Trust; PTR-14-297931 The Donald J. Amos GST Non-Exempt Trust.

The Court stated that it has no problem with the petitions to accept resignation of Trustee and appoint Successor Trustee but questioned why the bank was resigning. Mr. Wolfe stated that Bank of America does not administer special needs trusts. The Court granted said petitions (subject to review of the orders). Mr. Wolfe addressed the Court's concerns with respect to the tremendous amount of fees, allocation of costs for defending the Department of Developmental Services allegations regarding Douglas being charged to Donald's Trust, the accountings, provided history, explained services provided, case history and unforeseen circumstances to support modification of the trusts, etc. Mr. Der stated that funds need to be separated and allocated for Douglas' needs. The petitions to modify Donald's Trusts were denied as the Court does not find that there are extraordinary circumstances (Mr. Wolfe is to prepare the orders). The accounting petitions in all four Trusts were continued to January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Wolfe to file declaration/supplemental information indicating how to properly allocate the litigation cost. Mr. Wolfe stated that he will go back and look at the timing when fees were split and submit a revised order reflecting that this is not a final account as well as what is to be re-allocated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
